children.text: Life insurance is one of the most important financial decisions you'll make, yet many people find it confusing. This guide breaks down the essentials to help you get started with confidence.
What Is Life Insurance?

Life insurance is a contract between you and an insurance company. You pay premiums, and in return, the insurer provides a death benefit to your beneficiaries when you pass away. This financial safety net helps your loved ones cover expenses and maintain their quality of life.
Types of Life Insurance

Term Life Insurance provides coverage for a specific period—typically 10, 20, or 30 years. It's the most affordable option and ideal for covering temporary needs like a mortgage or raising children. If you outlive the term, the policy expires with no payout.
Whole Life Insurance offers lifelong coverage with a cash value component that grows over time. Premiums are higher, but the policy builds savings you can borrow against or withdraw.
Universal Life Insurance combines flexible premiums with a death benefit and cash value. You can adjust your coverage and payments as your needs change.
How Much Coverage Do You Need?
A common rule of thumb is 10-15 times your annual income. However, consider these factors:
Outstanding debts (mortgage, loans, credit cards)
Future expenses (children's education, daily living costs)
Existing savings and investments
Your spouse's earning potential
Key Terms to Know
Term
Definition
Premium
The amount you pay for coverage
Death Benefit
The payout your beneficiaries receive
Beneficiary
The person(s) who receive the death benefit
Cash Value
Savings component in permanent policies
Riders
Optional add-ons for extra coverage
Tips for First-Time Buyers
Start early. Premiums increase with age and health issues.
Compare quotes. Rates vary significantly between insurers.
Be honest on your application. Misrepresentation can lead to claim denial.
Review your policy regularly. Update coverage after major life events.
Consider a medical exam. Non-medical policies are convenient but often cost more.
Bottom Line

Life insurance isn't just about money—it's about peace of mind. Start by assessing your family's needs, compare your options, and choose a policy that fits your budget and goals. The right coverage today protects your loved ones tomorrow.

pages.title: About InsurHi - America's Trusted Insurance Platform

children.text: Insurance is one of the most important financial decisions you'll ever make—yet for millions of Americans, the process of finding the right coverage feels overwhelming, opaque, and unnecessarily complicated. Insurhi was built to change that.

We are an independent insurance comparison and education platform serving consumers across the United States. Our mission is simple but urgent: give every American the clear, unbiased information they need to protect themselves, their families, and their assets—without overpaying.
Who We Are

Insurhi is a team of licensed insurance professionals, certified financial planners, data analysts, and consumer advocates united by one belief: insurance shopping should be transparent, straightforward, and centered on the consumer—not the insurance company.

Our editorial and research team brings together over 75 years of combined experience in the insurance industry. Our experts have worked on the inside—as former underwriters at top carriers, claims adjusters who processed thousands of real claims, and licensed agents who sat across the table from real families making real decisions. That inside experience shapes everything we do, because we know exactly where the industry falls short—and where consumers get left behind.
What We Do

Insurhi provides comprehensive, side-by-side comparisons of insurance products across six major categories: Auto Insurance, Life Insurance, Home Insurance, Pet Insurance, Medicare Supplement Insurance, and Renters Insurance. Our database covers 60+ nationally recognized insurance providers, including State Farm, GEICO, Progressive, Allstate, USAA, Northwestern Mutual, Chubb, and many more.
But we go far beyond simple price comparison. Every provider on Insurhi is evaluated across multiple dimensions:
Financial strength
verified through A.M. Best, Moody's, and Standard & Poor's ratings
Customer satisfaction
measured by J.D. Power studies and NAIC complaint indices
Claims experience
assessed through proprietary research and verified customer reviews
Coverage depth
analyzed by our team to identify true value versus marketing spin
Discount availability
documented so you never leave money on the table
Our Editorial Integrity
We believe trust is earned, not claimed. That's why Insurhi maintains strict editorial independence from any insurance company or financial interest.
Our editorial policy guarantees:
No insurer can pay for a higher ranking or a favorable review
Our writers and editors are prohibited from accepting gifts, travel, or compensation from insurance companies
Every article undergoes fact-checking against primary sources before publication
All affiliate relationships and potential conflicts of interest are clearly disclosed
Content is reviewed and updated at minimum quarterly, and immediately when major policy or rate changes occur
When we recommend a product, it's because our research supports it—not because an insurer paid us to say so.
How We Help Consumers

Since our founding, Insurhi has helped hundreds of thousands of Americans navigate their insurance decisions with confidence. Our users consistently report saving $500 or more per year by comparing quotes and discovering discounts they didn't know existed.

We publish over 50 in-depth insurance guides, company reviews, and educational resources—all free and accessible to everyone. From first-time auto insurance buyers to seniors navigating Medicare Supplement options for the first time, our content is designed to meet people where they are with clear, actionable guidance.
Our Data and Methodology
Every comparison on Insurhi is grounded in rigorous, reproducible methodology. We collect data from:
Publicly available rate filings and state insurance department records
Third-party rating agencies (A.M. Best, J.D. Power, NAIC, Moody's)
Direct carrier research and verified policy documents
Consumer complaint databases and regulatory enforcement actions
Our rating methodology is published openly so you can understand exactly how we arrive at our recommendations. We don't hide behind vague claims of "expert picks"—we show our work.
How We're Funded

Transparency is non-negotiable. Insurhi may receive compensation when you click on certain links, request quotes, or purchase a policy through our platform. This is how we keep our content free for all users.

However, this compensation never influences our editorial content, rankings, or recommendations. The price you pay for insurance is the same whether you find a provider through Insurhi or contact them directly. We never sell your personal data, and we never prioritize a partner's interests over yours.
Our Commitment to You

Whether you're a first-time buyer trying to understand liability coverage, a parent comparing life insurance policies, or a senior evaluating Medigap plans during your Open Enrollment Period—Insurhi is here for you.
Our promise:
We will always put your interests first
We will always be honest about what we know and what we don't
We will always explain insurance in plain language, not jargon
We will always update our content when facts change
We will always disclose how we make money

Insurance protects the things that matter most. You deserve a resource that takes that responsibility as seriously as you do. That's what Insurhi delivers—every day, for every visitor, without exception.
